2026-07-06.patch_summary: This patch adds a sysctl (kernel.syscall_user_dispatch) to control whether userspace can arm syscall user dispatch via prctl or ptrace. The default value is 1, allowing arming of syscall user dispatch. Disabling the feature while it's already armed does not immediately deactivate it, but instead requires the user to attempt re-enabling via prctl or ptrace, which will fail if attempted again. This approach prevents undefined behavior in userland when translating non-Linux syscalls to Linux syscalls.
